H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Pebble (n=96, mean=7.1, std=1.82, confidence=strong) is the highest-performing Over-stimulation protocol with true right-skewed distribution (26 at score 8). Qualitative feedback (n=29) shows dominant self-listeners (16/29) reporting noticeable improvements settling at 10 minutes. Music opinion split (10 liked, 9 loved) indicates broad appeal with room for optimization.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble stands out for overstimulation relief with the highest positive response rate. Most listeners report noticeable calmness developing over 10 minutes, appreciating the music even if they don't love every aspect.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Rainfall (n=52, mean=6.83, std=1.84, confidence=strong) shows slightly lower efficacy with mid-range clustering. Qualitative data indicates slower entrainment (10-minute mode vs L1's 5 minutes). Music opinions show polarization (10 liked, 6 neutral), suggesting population-level differences in response to rhythmic elements.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall effectively eases overstimulation but settles more slowly than Pebble. Listeners are mixed on music taste with some finding it just okay, though rhythmically calming.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) achieves stable outcomes with tightly clustered distribution (std=1.68). Qualitative data shows balanced settle times (5-10 minutes) and overwhelmingly positive rhythm opinions (10 balanced, 10 very settling), suggesting well-tuned entrainment curve.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier provides consistent overstimulation relief with stable positive outcomes. Most feel balanced by it within 5-10 minutes, reporting very calming rhythm regardless of music opinion.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Meadow (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) demonstrates highest efficacy in Anxiety & Stress DESCENT tier with right-skewed distribution (long tail at higher scores). Qualitative data (n=23) shows mixed listener types (13 self, 6 clients, 4 supported) reporting moderate-to-significant improvements settling at 5-10 minutes. YouTube reach is minimal (views=5), suggesting narrow discovery despite strong efficacy.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Meadow helps reduce anxiety and stress for most listeners, especially those rating their own experience. People report noticeable calmer feelings settling within about 10 minutes. The music is generally appreciated, but we don't have broad reach data yet.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Cottage (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) shows consistent but less pronounced efficacy compared to L1. Distribution clusters in 7-9 range with moderate variability. Qualitative data reveals polarized music opinions despite strongly positive rhythm perception, suggesting population-level differences in music receptivity. YouTube view count remains low (views=6).
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage works well for anxiety but less consistently than Meadow. Most experience reduced stress over 5-20 minutes. Some find the music too neutral while others love it, showing different preferences in the same group.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Valley (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) maintains strong Anxiety & Stress outcomes with longer settle times (10-minute mode) consistent with phi-sigmoid curve for extended protocols. Qualitative feedback indicates 10/20 listeners settle near session end. Unique rhythm opinion profile shows high 'very settling' ratings compared to peer protocols.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley provides steady anxiety relief with deeper calm settling toward session end. People mostly report feeling noticeably better after about 10 minutes, with very calming rhythms. Music likes are mixed but positive overall.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Anchor (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.7, confidence=meaningful) reflects expected lower outcomes for Crisis DESCENT protocols. Distribution shifted toward mid-range scores (5-7) despite significant qualitative improvements reported. Notable clustering at scores 8-9 (12/43) contradicts lower mean, suggesting conservative scoring by distressed listeners. Settle times cluster at 5 minutes.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor brings crisis relief for most, with improvements noticeable within 5 minutes. Despite the lower average score (because incoming states are very distressed), listener feedback shows meaningful change - some feel significantly better quickly.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Harbor (n=35, mean=6.4, std=1.72, confidence=meaningful) performs comparably to L1 with similar outcome distribution. Qualitative data shows longer settle times (10-minute mode) consistent with deeper descent phase. Music opinion polarization (6 neutral, 5 loved) indicates potential population differences in protocol reception.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor offers moderate crisis relief with majority reporting improvements within 10 minutes. The music opinions are polarized - some really love the sound while others find it neutral.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Horizon (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) achieves highest Crisis tier mean with bimodal distribution (no scores 1-2, three scores at 10). Notable 7 immediate settle responses suggest fastest entrainment in crisis tier. Qualitative data shows mixed music opinions despite strong rhythm ratings.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon shows the fastest crisis response of the three with many feeling relief almost immediately. This immediate settling makes it stand out despite similar average scores to the other crisis protocols.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Lantern (n=55, mean=7.16, std=1.85, confidence=strong) delivers highest Sleep & Rest efficacy with strong right-skew (11 at score 9). Qualitative data shows immediate/5-minute settle dominance (8/15) despite longer protocol length. Music opinions are overwhelmingly positive (10 liked, 3 loved) with no neutral responses.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern helps with sleep and rest impressively, with many scoring it highly. Listeners settle quickly, often within 5 minutes, and overwhelmingly like the music and rhythm experience.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful) shows tightest distribution in Sleep tier (std=1.55). Unusual listener_type composition (only 4 self-listeners in n=15 qualitative sample) raises questions about clinician/proxy rating biases compared to self-reported experiences. Rhythm shows highest 'too busy' responses in tier.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock similarly aids sleep but there's a notable split: about half report through caregivers, and some find the rhythm too busy despite good outcomes. This might indicate different listener experiences.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight (n=29, mean=7.1, std=1.65, confidence=meaningful) maintains high efficacy with balanced outcome distribution. Qualitative data reveals fastest entrainment in tier (8/22 settle at 5 minutes), contrary to expected phi-sigmoid onset for longer protocols. Music opinion includes notable neutral responses (6/22) not seen in L1/L2.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight supports sleep well with outcomes matching Lantern despite lower views. Many feel sleepy within 5 minutes unexpectedly quickly for this session length, and music opinions are less uniformly positive.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful) registers lowest in Focus & Clarity with notably 'sliced' distribution. Qualitative data indicates rapid onset (6 immediate, 7 at 5 minutes) but rhythm opinions marked 'slightly busy' (9/23). This misalignment between fast entrainment and rhythm complexity suggests optimization potential.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass aids focus and clarity but less effectively than its counterparts. Some listeners feel slightly overwhelmed (reporting busy rhythm), though many still notice improvements by session end.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.6, confidence=meaningful) shows marked improvement over L1 with right-shifted distribution. Qualitative data (n=17) shows slower onset (9 at 10 minutes) and balanced rhythm opinions. Music reception is polarized (6 liked, 6 loved) despite strong outcomes.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint significantly improves focus with higher scores and faster settling than L1. Most report moderate to significant gains over 10 minutes with varied music opinions.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit (n=34, mean=7.56, std=1.96, confidence=meaningful) achieves highest series mean with strong right-tail concentration (9 at score 10). Qualitative data (n=29) shows strongest change ratings (11 significant) and longest settle times (15 at 10 minutes), indicating deep entrainment. High variance (std=1.96) suggests population subgroup effects.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit takes the lead for focus enhancement with the highest outcome scores. Listeners consistently report noticeable to significant improvements, especially when staying through the full session.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Ember (n=35, mean=6.74, std=1.7, confidence=meaningful) shows moderate efficacy for creative flow with clustered distribution. Qualitative data (n=21) shows distributed improvement ratings (8 slight, 5 moderate, 5 significant). Music reception is positive (9 liked, 6 loved) despite outcome score suggesting room for optimization.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember aids creative flow moderately based on reported improvements. Settle times vary widely from immediate to 10 minutes, with some feeling only slight enhancement despite enjoying the music.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Current (n=25, mean=6.72, std=1.43, confidence=meaningful) achieves tightest outcome variance in Creative Flow (std=1.43). Qualitative data shows balanced improvement ratings and overwhelmingly positive music opinions (6 loved, 6 liked). Settle times favor 10 minutes (5/15), consistent with HOLD design intent.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current offers consistent creative flow support with balanced outcomes. People settle over 5-10 minutes valuing the music and rhythm, feeling moderately enhanced in creativity.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing) shows highest Creative Flow efficacy with compact distribution. Qualitative data (n=25) reveals balanced settle times and polarized music opinions (9 liked, 7 neutral). The small n limits confidence, but this protocol may benefit from scaling as a template.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora excels as the creative flow leader with highest scores, but too few listeners have tried it. Those who have give mixed music feedback despite strong creative boosts.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stone (n=26, mean=7.08, std=1.32, confidence=meaningful) shows stable outcomes with lowest variance in Maintenance tier. Qualitative data reveals longest settle times (9 at 20 minutes) consistent with HOLD design. Music opinions are mixed (6 liked, 6 neutral), suggesting rhythm drives outcomes more than auditory elements.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone supports balance maintenance with stable outcomes. It requires longer settling (about 20 minutes) and people are mixed on music despite valuing the balanced rhythm experience.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Axis (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful) registers highest variance in tier with lower mean. Distribution spread (scores 4-9) suggests population subgroups with different baseline states. Qualitative data shows shorter settle times (6 at 5 minutes) indicating less consistent entrainment.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis helps balance but with more variable results compared to L1. Settle times are faster but outcomes are less consistent, suggesting different listener needs.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit (n=14, mean=6.86, std=1.7, confidence=developing) maintains moderate efficacy with unusual distribution (8 at score 7, truncated right tail). Notably high non-settle rate (4/14) suggests design issues for certain listeners. Qualitative data indicates fastest onset but highest dropout in tier.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit moderately supports balance but with some listeners not settling at all. The few who have tried it often feel balanced after about 5 minutes, though we lack broader data.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze (n=38, mean=6.68, std=1.93, confidence=meaningful) shows moderate Mood & Energy outcomes with central distribution. Qualitative data reveals mixed change ratings (7 moderate, 6 significant) despite balanced outcomes. Longest settle times (6 at 20 minutes) suggest slow entrainment curve.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze moderately boosts mood and energy for many settling over 20 minutes. Balance of improvement reports (significant to no change) suggests varied listener experiences.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise (n=24, mean=6.71, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) maintains similar efficacy to L1 with lower variance. Qualitative data shows balanced onset (4 immediate, 4 at 20 minutes) and polarized music opinions (7 liked, 6 loved). Notably fast onset responses (4 immediate) suggest effective early entrainment.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise similarly boosts mood energy with mixed outcomes. Some feel significant improvements immediately or by 20 minutes, appreciating the music despite variable results.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft (n=17, mean=6.24, std=1.79, confidence=developing) shows lowest Mood mean with moderate variance. Qualitative data indicates inconsistent improvements (9 noticeable, 5 no effect) and fastest onset (8 at 5 minutes) suggesting possible premature entrainment. Music opinions polarized (11 liked, 7 loved).
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft shows moderate mood energy support but the lowest in its series. Many notice benefits within 5 minutes, though a significant portion don't feel different, possibly indicating state mismatch.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath (n=21, mean=6.24, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful) achieves moderate Motivation & Drive outcomes with right-skewed distribution. Qualitative data indicates immediate settling (9/17) and balanced music/rhythm opinions. Lower mean suggests protocol may be missing key elements for motivation-specific neural entrainment.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath moderately aids motivation drive with noticeable improvements reported by some within 5 minutes. Few feel significant boosts but most experience at least some positive change.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail (n=14, mean=6.0, std=1.57, confidence=developing) shows moderate efficacy with tight variance. Distribution shows limited upper tail (only 1 at score 9). Qualitative data reveals slowest onset (5 at 20 minutes) in TRANSFORMING series. Music opinions polarized (9 loved, 5 neutral) despite modest outcomes.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail offers moderate motivation support but slower than other protocols. Listeners need about 20 minutes for noticeable effects - longer than typical - though music is well-received.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback (n=12, mean=6.92, std=2.31, confidence=developing) achieves highest Motivation mean with wide distribution. Qualitative data shows marked variability: 8 moderate improvement vs 4/12 reporting no/lower improvement. Rhythm opinions are evenly split between 'balanced' and 'busy', suggesting overlap with FUSS protocols.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback provides the best motivation support of its trio with varied results. Some feel significantly more driven after 5 minutes, while others don't notice change, possibly due to different starting states.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern (n=23, mean=6.65, std=1.58, confidence=meaningful) shows moderate Mind & Clarity outcomes with central distribution. Qualitative feedback (n=25) includes 2 reports of worsening despite overall positive change ratings. Rhythm opinions cluster at 'balanced/comfy' (9) with notable 'loved music' responses (9).
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern helps mind clarity for most listeners noticeably, though a couple felt worse. It takes about 10 minutes to feel balanced with most appreciating the rhythm and music.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight (n=15, mean=6.6, std=1.45, confidence=developing) maintains consistent efficacy with tight distribution. Qualitative data shows fastest onset (6 immediate, 6 at 10 minutes) and polarized music opinions (6 loved, 6 liked). High 'very settling' rhythm ratings (12) suggest rhythm drives outcomes.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight similarly supports mind clarity with very calming rhythms. Settling happens quickly for many, though too few listeners have tried it for reliable patterns.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos (n=9, mean=5.33, std=2.29, confidence=developing) shows lowest mean across all protocols with high variance. Distribution is bimodal (cluster at 2-3 and 9-10). Notable inconsistency in qualitative feedback (3 no effect, 3 moderate improvement). This suggests population mismatch or design issues.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos shows mixed results for mind clarity with the lowest average score. The few listeners are divided between feeling unchanged and moderately better, suggesting it might need redesign.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The is a BACKUP dataset. The automatic run may have failed. This data is recent and contains 1,000 outcome scores across 30 protocols, with three series demonstrating distinct statistical profiles. THRIVING protocols outperform others with the highest mean score (6.94) and low variance, reflecting consistent listener benefits. HEALING protocols show robust stability (mean 6.95), while TRANSFORMING exhibits greater spread (std=1.83) as expected for ascending-arc designs.
• THRIVING leads with highest mean and tightest variance, suggesting consistently reliable efficacy.
• HEALING protocols show stable outcomes across DESCENT designs, with crisis protocols scoring lower as expected.
• TRANSFORMING carries highest volatility, consistent with low-affect state interventions and mixed protocol types.
• Crisis protocols (H-CR) score structurally lower (mean ~6.4) but show significant qualitative improvements.
• Focus & Clarity protocols (T-FC) in THRIVING excel, notably Summit at 7.56 mean.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
The three series reflect distinct efficacy signatures aligned with their design intentions. HEALING protocols achieve stable outcomes through DESCENT designs focused on regulation, crisis protocols show lower but clinically meaningful improvements. THRIVING protocols deliver the highest efficacy through HOLD designs that optimize cognitive states. TRANSFORMING shows the largest variance due to mixed ARC approaches and low-affect targeting.
• HEALING: Strongest stability (std=1.78) with consistent outcomes in DESCENT protocols, crisis designs score lower.
• THRIVING: Highest mean (6.94) with creative flow holding steady despite moderate variance, focus protocols lead the series.
• TRANSFORMING: High volatility (std=1.83) across mixed types, with DESCENT protocols outperforming ASCENT variations.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols deviate substantially from series norms. X-MC-L3 shows the lowest mean in the dataset (5.33) with notable variance. X-MD-L2 underperforms within its ASCENT design context. H-SL-L2 reveals unusual listener_type composition suggesting population shifts.
• X-MC-L3 (mean=5.33, n=9): Lowest outcome in dataset suggests potential protocol mismatch for mind clarity interventions.
• X-MD-L2 (mean=6.0, n=14): ASCENT protocol underperforming within series, possibly needing design adjustment.
• H-SL-L2 skewed listener types (73% proxy respondents) may reflect population dynamics affecting outcome interpretation.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Multiple analyzers converge on key protocol redesign needs. X-MC-L3 shows consistent anomalies requiring review. Summit's success offers template potential. Listener composition shifts in H-SL-L2 warrant investigation. Over-stimulation protocols show performance tiering.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 warrants redesign for better mind clarity impact (n=9).
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 (Summit) should template underperforming ASCENT variants (mean=7.56).
• H-SL-L2 listener_type skew (73% proxy) warrants population dynamics investigation.
• H-OS-L1 outperforms variants— investigate parameter differences (n=96, mean=7.10).
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
CROSS-RUN DRIFT indicates analytical evolution rather than data instability, with all protocols showing coherence drift in 3-run history. SEMANTIC CLUSTERS reveal strong grouping between creative flow variants (T-CF-L1/L2) and isolation patterns for certain protocols. H-SL-L1 and T-MB-L3 show consistently distinct signatures.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: All 30 protocols show analytical drift (sim<0.85) across 3 runs—reflects evolving commentary not data instability.
• SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Creative Flow protocols (T-CF-L1/L2) cluster tightly (sim=0.895); H-SL-L1 and T-MB-L3 are consistently isolated.
• Confidence note: Low reliability due to limited run history (only 3 tracked runs).

Qualitative data reveals mixed change ratings (7 moderate, 6 significant) despite balanced outcomes. Longest settle times (6 at 20 minutes) suggest slow entrainment curve.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: Sunrise (n=24, mean=6.71, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) maintains similar efficacy to L1 with lower variance. Qualitative data shows balanced onset (4 immediate, 4 at 20 minutes) and polarized music opinions (7 liked, 6 loved). Notably fast onset responses (4 immediate) suggest effective early entrainment.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: Updraft (n=17, mean=6.24, std=1.79, confidence=developing) shows lowest Mood mean with moderate variance. Qualitative data indicates inconsistent improvements (9 noticeable, 5 no effect) and fastest onset (8 at 5 minutes) suggesting possible premature entrainment. Music opinions polarized (11 liked, 7 loved).

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Meadow helps reduce anxiety and stress for most listeners, especially those rating their own experience. People report noticeable calmer feelings settling within about 10 minutes. The music is generally appreciated, but we don't have broad reach data yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Cottage works well for anxiety but less consistently than Meadow. Most experience reduced stress over 5-20 minutes. Some find the music too neutral while others love it, showing different preferences in the same group.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Valley provides steady anxiety relief with deeper calm settling toward session end. People mostly report feeling noticeably better after about 10 minutes, with very calming rhythms. Music likes are mixed but positive overall.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Anchor brings crisis relief for most, with improvements noticeable within 5 minutes. Despite the lower average score (because incoming states are very distressed), listener feedback shows meaningful change - some feel significantly better quickly.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Harbor offers moderate crisis relief with majority reporting improvements within 10 minutes. The music opinions are polarized - some really love the sound while others find it neutral.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Horizon shows the fastest crisis response of the three with many feeling relief almost immediately. This immediate settling makes it stand out despite similar average scores to the other crisis protocols.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Pebble stands out for overstimulation relief with the highest positive response rate. Most listeners report noticeable calmness developing over 10 minutes, appreciating the music even if they don't love every aspect.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Rainfall effectively eases overstimulation but settles more slowly than Pebble. Listeners are mixed on music taste with some finding it just okay, though rhythmically calming.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Glacier provides consistent overstimulation relief with stable positive outcomes. Most feel balanced by it within 5-10 minutes, reporting very calming rhythm regardless of music opinion.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Lantern helps with sleep and rest impressively, with many scoring it highly. Listeners settle quickly, often within 5 minutes, and overwhelmingly like the music and rhythm experience.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Hammock similarly aids sleep but there's a notable split: about half report through caregivers, and some find the rhythm too busy despite good outcomes. This might indicate different listener experiences.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Midnight supports sleep well with outcomes matching Lantern despite lower views. Many feel sleepy within 5 minutes unexpectedly quickly for this session length, and music opinions are less uniformly positive.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Ember aids creative flow moderately based on reported improvements. Settle times vary widely from immediate to 10 minutes, with some feeling only slight enhancement despite enjoying the music.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Current offers consistent creative flow support with balanced outcomes. People settle over 5-10 minutes valuing the music and rhythm, feeling moderately enhanced in creativity.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Aurora excels as the creative flow leader with highest scores, but too few listeners have tried it. Those who have give mixed music feedback despite strong creative boosts.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Compass aids focus and clarity but less effectively than its counterparts. Some listeners feel slightly overwhelmed (reporting busy rhythm), though many still notice improvements by session end.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Waypoint significantly improves focus with higher scores and faster settling than L1. Most report moderate to significant gains over 10 minutes with varied music opinions.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Summit takes the lead for focus enhancement with the highest outcome scores. Listeners consistently report noticeable to significant improvements, especially when staying through the full session.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Stone supports balance maintenance with stable outcomes. It requires longer settling (about 20 minutes) and people are mixed on music despite valuing the balanced rhythm experience.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Axis helps balance but with more variable results compared to L1. Settle times are faster but outcomes are less consistent, suggesting different listener needs.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Orbit moderately supports balance but with some listeners not settling at all. The few who have tried it often feel balanced after about 5 minutes, though we lack broader data.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Cavern helps mind clarity for most listeners noticeably, though a couple felt worse. It takes about 10 minutes to feel balanced with most appreciating the rhythm and music.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Starlight similarly supports mind clarity with very calming rhythms. Settling happens quickly for many, though too few listeners have tried it for reliable patterns.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Cosmos shows mixed results for mind clarity with the lowest average score. The few listeners are divided between feeling unchanged and moderately better, suggesting it might need redesign.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Footpath moderately aids motivation drive with noticeable improvements reported by some within 5 minutes. Few feel significant boosts but most experience at least some positive change.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Trail offers moderate motivation support but slower than other protocols. Listeners need about 20 minutes for noticeable effects - longer than typical - though music is well-received.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Switchback provides the best motivation support of its trio with varied results. Some feel significantly more driven after 5 minutes, while others don't notice change, possibly due to different starting states.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Breeze moderately boosts mood and energy for many settling over 20 minutes. Balance of improvement reports (significant to no change) suggests varied listener experiences.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Sunrise similarly boosts mood energy with mixed outcomes. Some feel significant improvements immediately or by 20 minutes, appreciating the music despite variable results.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Updraft shows moderate mood energy support but the lowest in its series. Many notice benefits within 5 minutes, though a significant portion don't feel different, possibly indicating state mismatch.
